mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0%, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations and consumer spending acceleration that lifts corporate revenues and equity prices.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below the 8.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based demand expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in HOUST and RSAFS fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which compresses input cost inflation and supports near-term equity outperformance.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises consistently correlate with negative 5-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and nonfarm payroll employment confirms a self-reinforcing economic expansion, driving sustained corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Combined positive growth in GDPC1 and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand, which translates into higher aggregate consumption and positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with PAYEMS momentum over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output without concurrent price increases indicates benign supply-side expansion, which improves profit margins and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL stays below 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive supply shocks fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns when inflation expectations remain anchored.
